  "sources": "The code reads command-line arguments (output_path, options), user-supplied options for QR code customization, and the QR code data generated by QRCodeNode().",
  "sinks": "The writeFile function writes the generated QR code data to a file at the specified output path. No other sinks directly interact with untrusted data.",
  "flows": "Command-line arguments -> setOptions() with user options -> draw() generates QR code data -> fs.writeFile() saves data to disk.",
  "anomalies": "The options object 'opt' is received as a string from yargs, which might be intended to be an object but is defaulted to an empty object. The code does not parse 'opt' string into an object, possibly leading to misbehavior. No hardcoded credentials or suspicious code present. No obfuscation detected. The QRCodeNode module is external; its security depends on its implementation.",
  "analysis": "The script uses yargs to parse CLI arguments, with options for QR code customization. The handleDefaultCommand function creates a QR code instance, sets options, and attempts to generate a QR code image. The QR code data is then saved to a specified file. The main concern is the 'opt' parameter; it is configured as a string but passed directly to setOptions(), which may not accept a string, potentially causing runtime issues. No network connections, data exfiltration, or malicious code are present. The code appears to be a straightforward CLI tool with no suspicious activity.",
  "conclusion": "The code is a simple QR code generator CLI script with no malicious intent. The main risk is improper handling of the 'opt' parameter, which may cause errors but not security issues. No malware or security threats are identified.",
